name: fe2_minimal
drl: rule K salience 7 when $b : E2(ts == 0) P() then end
_finding: FLAG-EAGER ARC minimal witness (D-179): 2 types, 1 rule — K@7 `E2(ts==0) P()`, E2(0)@epoch0, advance 51, advance 50 + P(1) (clock 101 == deadline 101: flagged, alive). Oracle 3x: [] (flag-eager refuses the pair). Engine: [K] — the corpse E2(0)'s LEFT-fold was deferred by path unlinking to the same pop eval as the fresh right; the left walk corpse-checks only its right partner P(1). MECHANISM (D-179, trace-pinned): the plain-join insert walks corpse-check only the PARTNER side (right walk: l.iter().any(is_expired) on left partners, phreak.rs ~1930; left walk: is_expired(f) on right partners, ~1954) — the WALKING fact/tuple is never flag-checked. A corpse whose own memory-fold is deferred past its flagging — unlinked-path staging accumulation (D-101/fz_7_145) or the flush right-stash (D-102) — walks in flagged and forms pairs. The temporal arm does NOT share the blind spot (fe7 green). Fix shape (NOT ported, Bryan gate): corpse-guard the partner LOOP of both plain walks (memory push stays — flag-eager, retraction-lazy); check the AB-arm sites (~595/612, same partner-only pattern, no witness yet) at port time.
